mission.title: Install and Configure Langflow
mission.description: Langflow is a powerful platform for building and deploying AI-powered agents and workflows, providing a visual authoring experience and built-in API servers. It is ideal for developers looking to integrate AI workflows into applications without managing complex dependencies. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential resource overhead.

steps.title: Install the Langflow CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Langflow CLI tool, which is necessary for managing Langflow deployments.

```bash

pip install langflow==1.8.0

```

> Ensure Python 3.10–3.13 is installed.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in the desired region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create langflow-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region as necessary.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials langflow-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Deploy Langflow
steps.description:
This command deploys Langflow using Helm, which simplifies the installation process.

```bash

helm repo add langflow https://charts.langflow.org

helm install langflow langflow/langflow --version 1.8.0 --namespace langflow --create-namespace

```

> Add the Langflow Helm repository.
> Deploy Langflow in a dedicated namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the Langflow deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment langflow --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10 --namespace langflow

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml

```

> Set up autoscaling based on CPU usage.
> Ensure you have a monitoring configuration file ready.

steps.title: Cost Warning
steps.description: Be aware that running a Kubernetes cluster incurs costs based on the resources allocated.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Langflow
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Langflow deployment and associated resources. Be cautious as this may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall langflow --namespace langflow

gcloud container clusters delete langflow-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This removes the Langflow deployment.
> This deletes the entire cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Langflow
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your data and configurations. This process will update Langflow to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl cp langflow-config:/path/to/config /local/path/to/backup

helm upgrade langflow langflow/langflow --version 1.8.0 --namespace langflow

kubectl get pods --namespace langflow

```

> Backup your configurations.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Verify that all pods are running and healthy after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials langflow-cluster --region=us-central1' to reset kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the Langflow deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or resize existing nodes in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if there are issues with the application configuration or dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace langflow' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Helm release for Langflow has not been installed or has been deleted.

**Fix:**
Reinstall Langflow using 'helm install langflow langflow/langflow --version 1.8.0 --namespace langflow'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI tools and improved the visual builder interface.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API endpoints for better performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.7.0, ensure to update your configurations to match the new API structure.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' when running 'kubectl get pods --namespace langflow'. All pods should be in 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
